title: Claim-Receipt
description: An open, decentralized provenance format for a single claim. A claim carries its own verifiable cognitive state, the observation that grounds it, and links to evidence and to other claims. Designed to be adopted and linked-into without a central service. Cognitive-state vocabulary is shared with the Cognition Track graph (survived-stress-test / speculative / raw) on purpose — do not fork a second wordlist. NON-GOAL: this is a protocol layer, not a rating agency; there is deliberately no field that scores a person.

id.description: Stable identifier for this claim (e.g. a memory id, a slug, or a content hash). Lets others link-into the claim.

claim.description: The assertion itself, stated so it could in principle be wrong.

cognitive_state.description: Shared vocabulary with Cognition Track. survived-stress-test = has withstood a real attempt to break it (cite the test); speculative = reasoned but untested; raw = unfiltered intake, not yet examined. A speculative/raw claim is an idea, NOT a result.

cognitive_state_reason.description: One line: why this state and not a stronger/weaker one. Names the stress test for survived, or names what is untested for speculative/raw.

confidence.description: Subjective probability the claim survives further scrutiny. A number, not a score of any person. Honesty of the distribution is the load-bearing constraint — do not inflate to look finished.

observed.description: What was actually observed or measured, kept distinct from the claim it is taken to support. Empty/absent for a speculative claim with no observation behind it yet — and that absence is itself informative.

provenance.description: Where this claim came from and how it was derived.

source.description: Origin: a paper, an experiment, a conversation, a prior claim id, etc.

derivation_type.description: empirical = grounded in observation/experiment; reasoned = argued from premises; compiled = synthesized from multiple prior claims; intake = raw external input; standing-policy = holds by construction/guardrail, not as a falsifiable hypothesis.

evidence.description: Receipt/falsification links: pointers to the test, data, or refutation that bears on this claim. A claim with an empty evidence array is honestly an untested one.

kind.description: Direction of the evidence with respect to the claim. falsifies / fails-to-reproduce are first-class — a surviving graph keeps its refuted nodes visible.

ref.description: URL, citation, or claim id of the evidence.

edges.description: Relations to OTHER claims, never to people. This is how credit grows bottom-up: a claim is supported or refuted by other claims, not conferred top-down.

relation.description: Edge semantics, shared with the Cognition Track graph.
